text: BREAKING — LEBANON: Israeli drone strikes hit convoy on Jiyeh coastal highway south of Beirut, killing 8 including 2 children. First strikes in Beirut area since April 16 ceasefire. Lebanon ceasefire expires May 17 — 4 days away. Lebanese Health Ministry confirms 2,897 killed since Feb 28. Hezbollah vows 'proportional response.'
verificationNote: Al Jazeera liveblog and Reuters confirmed Jiyeh highway strikes and casualty count. Lebanese Health Ministry official figures.

text: HEZBOLLAH: Largest-ever drone swarm launched against northern Israel — hundreds of fiber-optic FPV drones in coordinated waves. IDF confirms 2 soldiers wounded. Drones are unjammable due to fiber-optic guidance (no RF signal to intercept). IDF still has no counter-drone solution. Alma Center confirms new tactic represents 'paradigm shift' in asymmetric warfare.
verificationNote: Times of Israel, Jerusalem Post, and Alma Center weekly report confirmed drone swarm details. IDF spokesperson confirmed 2 wounded.

text: GAZA: IDF confirms Mohammed Sinwar — brother of slain Hamas leader Yahya Sinwar — killed in tunnel operation in southern Gaza. IDF says he was directing remaining Hamas military operations from underground network. Hamas confirms death, vows 'the resistance does not depend on one man.' Escalation risk as Hamas hardliners consolidate.
verificationNote: IDF official statement confirmed. Times of Israel and CNN confirmed Hamas acknowledgment. Reuters confirmed tunnel operation details.

text: NUCLEAR: Energy Secretary Wright says Iran is 'frighteningly close' to nuclear weapons — strongest warning from a cabinet official yet. ISW assessment: Iran preparing infrastructure for renewed hostilities post-ceasefire, restoring 30 of 33 Hormuz mine-laying sites. Iran retains 70% of pre-war missile arsenal. IRGC launches 'Martyr Commander' exercise near Tehran.
verificationNote: Reuters and CBS News confirmed Wright quote. ISW daily assessment confirmed Iranian military preparations. IRGC exercise confirmed by Tasnim News.

text: ECONOMY: PPI surges 6.0% YoY — war-driven energy costs now accelerating producer inflation alongside 3.8% CPI. S&P 500 hits 7,444 record and Nasdaq 26,402 record despite inflation fears. IEA emergency report: global oil supply deficit 1.78M bpd — worst since 2011 Libya crisis. Brent $108.07 (+35.4%), WTI $101.59 (+34.6%). Gas $4.511/gal.
verificationNote: BLS PPI release confirmed. Bloomberg and CNBC confirmed S&P/Nasdaq records. IEA report confirmed supply deficit figure.

text: DIPLOMACY: Trump in Beijing tells reporters 'deal or decimated' — Vance says 'making progress.' UAE officially denies Netanyahu secret visit claim despite multiple confirmations. Israeli opposition files Knesset dissolution bill citing 'government incompetence.' Russia test-fires Sarmat ICBM. WaPo intel leak: China exploiting war to expand Pacific influence. Ceasefire expires May 17.
verificationNote: ABC News and Fox News confirmed Trump quote. CNN confirmed Vance quote. Times of Israel confirmed UAE denial and Knesset bill. WaPo confirmed China intelligence assessment.

text: LEBANON SOUTH: Heavy fighting continues across southern Lebanon — 7 additional killed in IDF strikes on villages south of the Litani River. Total Day 75 Lebanon dead now at least 15. 56 additional injured. Paramedic killed in targeted ambulance strike near Nabatieh. Lebanon ceasefire expiration May 17 raising alarm — UN peacekeepers reinforcing positions.
verificationNote: Al Jazeera Day 75 liveblog confirmed casualty figures. Lebanese Red Cross confirmed paramedic death. UNIFIL statement confirmed reinforcement.

text: INTEL: US intelligence has assessed Iran retains 70% of its pre-war missile stockpile and has restored operational access to 30 of its 33 missile sites along the Strait of Hormuz — indicating Tehran retains substantial capability to threaten US warships and commercial tankers. ISW assesses Iran is actively preparing for resumption of hostilities.
verificationNote: Euronews and Jerusalem Post confirmed US intelligence assessment. ISW daily update confirmed Iranian military preparations.

text: INTEL LEAK: A classified Joint Staff analysis prepared for Gen. Caine found China is exploiting the Iran war to gain advantage across military, economic, and diplomatic domains — acquiring data on US operational patterns and filling energy supply gaps created by the Hormuz blockade. Intel assessment rated 'high confidence.'
verificationNote: Washington Post confirmed classified Joint Staff analysis via leak, May 13.

text: GAP: CNN analysis finds significant contradiction between what Trump says publicly about Iran's military weakness and what US intelligence privately assesses — Iran retains substantial missile capability and can economically outlast the blockade for months. Hegseth told Congress opposite things in classified vs. public settings on US ability to reopen Hormuz by force.
verificationNote: CNN analysis published May 14, confirmed by multiple national security reporters.

text: ASSESSMENT: Iran's 'Hormuz gambit' has exposed the limits of US military power — while the US can deter outright Iranian aggression, forcing a reopening of the Strait by military means alone risks disproportionate escalation and regional blowback from the GCC. The only viable path is diplomatic pressure via China and economic pain.
verificationNote: CSIS published analysis on Hormuz gambit and limits of US military power.

text: DEAL GAP: Tehran's 14-point counter-proposal and Washington's 14-point demand document remain far apart on sequencing of nuclear concessions vs. sanctions relief. US demands 20-year enrichment moratorium plus surrender of 440kg enriched uranium; Iran offers 5 years. Both sides calculate the other will blink first.
verificationNote: TIME analysis of US-Iran deal gap confirmed. Axios confirmed 'one-page memo' framework efforts.

text: LEBANON ESCALATION: Israel struck 40+ locations in Lebanon on May 14, killing 22 including 8 children — the deadliest single day for Lebanon since the April 17 ceasefire. Israel-Lebanon peace talks opened at the US State Department the same day, creating acute diplomatic tension. Over 417 killed in Lebanon since the ceasefire took effect.
verificationNote: Free Malaysia Today confirmed 22 killed. RTE News confirmed Israel-Lebanon talks opening.

text: BRICS DIPLOMACY: Iran FM Araghchi's presence at BRICS foreign ministers' meeting in New Delhi (alongside India, Russia, Saudi Arabia, Egypt) represents Iran's most significant multilateral diplomatic engagement since the war began. Iran and UAE — currently on opposite sides of the conflict — are both BRICS members attending the same forum, making this a uniquely tense diplomatic juncture.
verificationNote: Al Jazeera confirmed BRICS meeting significance. Tribune India confirmed Araghchi arrival.

text: DETERRENCE: With Iran retaining 70% of its missile arsenal and restoring 30 of 33 Hormuz missile sites, Haaretz analysts assess Trump's deterrence has effectively been 'squandered' — Trump may feel forced to resume war operations if ceasefire collapses on May 17. The Iran-Israel ceasefire has a three-day extension window, but both Trump and Iran's First VP suggest neither side will back down.
verificationNote: Haaretz published analysis on Trump's squandered Iran deterrence, May 12.
